01 / About

Grounded in roots,
driven by growth.

I graduated from Fordham with a major in Finance and a minor in Economics. I've built skills in financial analysis and data interpretation, and I'm genuinely passionate about the dynamics of markets and what drives value creation in the finance industry.

Growing up, I spent countless hours at my parents' small jewelry shop in New York. Helping with simple tasks taught me the importance of customer relationships and the value of hard work. Those experiences sparked my interest in business operations and finance, which shaped my appreciation for both the analytical and interpersonal sides of the industry.

Today I want to leverage that combination: rigor with numbers, and the human side of building meaningful connections. It's the same instinct whether I'm reading the tape before the open or partnering with a client to solve a problem.

02 / Experience

Selected experience

Territory Account Associate · Techtronic Industries (TTI)
JAN 2026 – JUNE 2026
New York, NY · Full-time
  • Partnered with store KDMs to support merchandising, account execution, and sales strategy across TTI brands, overseeing a portfolio with $356,445 in sales and 10,241 units sold while improving sell-through, inventory productivity, and in-stock conditions.
  • Leveraged Power BI, POS, and sales analytics tools to monitor KPI performance, benchmark results against division comp (7.08%) and national comp (8.02%), and identify opportunities to optimize on-shelf availability, SKU productivity, and category placement.
  • Drove brand visibility and category growth across Milwaukee, Ryobi, and RIDGID by prioritizing high-velocity SKUs and supporting promotional execution, with Milwaukee driving 79% of total sales and standout growth in Milwaukee Outdoor Power Equipment (+186%), RIDGID Cordless (+39%), and Ryobi Pressure Washers (+35%).
Founder & Creative Lead · Lancier
SEP 2025 – PRESENT
Brooklyn, NY · Founder-led venture
  • Founded a silver jewelry e-commerce brand as a new digital line under the family jewelry business, building it end-to-end over 8 months while leading a 3-person team across brand, web, and operations.
  • Built a structured inventory and pricing system cataloguing 361 items at $97.6K retail value ($3.1K cost basis), engineering a weight-based pricing model (20× multiplier) and coded SKU framework across 8 product lines to standardize listings and analyze stock through pivot tables.
  • Designed a direct-to-consumer website across 5 product categories and 10+ page templates, defined the full brand identity around a "jewelry as protective armor" positioning, and launched the storefront on a Shopify / Cloudflare / Railway stack.
Retail Operations Manager · Golden Palace Jewelry
JUN 2025 – DEC 2025
New York, NY · Part-time
  • Led FP&A for $100K+ in monthly sales, refining forecasting and budgeting processes to support growth.
  • Managed AI-driven inventory initiatives and oversaw vendor negotiations across multiple store locations.
  • Conducted advanced reconciliations and POS audits, driving month-over-month increases in revenue and customer retention.
  • Supervised and trained new hires, implementing efficient, data-driven team workflows.

New York, NY · On-site

03b / Selected wins

Two problems, in depth

Research & Analytics · MetroPlusHealth

Helping flag high-risk members earlier

Summer 2024 · Behavioral Health team
ChallengeThe Behavioral Health team needed a better way to identify high-risk HARP members from a sprawling set of healthcare records, accuracy and compliance were non-negotiable.
What I didI used SQL to manage and organize a dataset of more than a million records, verified 500+ credentialing records with a zero error rate, and supported the analysis that fed a patient risk-scoring model.
+20%improvement in identifying high-risk members
FP&A & Operations · Golden Palace Jewelry

Tighter forecasts, leaner inventory

2025 · $100K+ monthly volume
ChallengeA multi-location retail business needed more accurate forecasting and smarter inventory planning to support growth while controlling waste and cost.
What I didI owned FP&A across six-figure monthly volume, refined the forecasting and budgeting assumptions, ran reconciliations and POS audits, and managed AI-driven inventory initiatives and vendor negotiations.
+20%forecast accuracy, with month-over-month revenue gains

Designing the mark

The Lancier sigil was built up in stages with my team — starting from the tip of a lance, evolving into a gothic arch, then balanced internal forms and a final diamond detail. A small emblem for the "jewelry as protective armor" idea: carry the lance.

04c / Market views

Where I have conviction

I follow the market closely as an active retail investor. These are the themes I keep coming back to — and the way I try to act on them: build a long-term ETF core, express single-name views with patience, and let tiered limit orders do the buying instead of chasing.

AI & semiconductorsCompute

The compute layer of the AI buildout — the picks-and-shovels of accelerated computing and the foundries that make it possible.

NVDAAMDAVGOTSM
Power & nuclearEnergy

AI's real bottleneck is electricity. Data centers need reliable baseload power, which puts independent generators and the nuclear fuel cycle back in focus.

CEGVSTCCJURNM
Financials & exchangesRates

A higher-for-longer world and steady market volume favor large banks and the exchanges that earn on activity regardless of direction.

BACCBOE
Gold & fintech railsHedge · Access

Gold as a real-asset hedge against debasement, paired with the fintech platforms broadening how a new generation invests.

IAUHOOD
How I invest

A diversified ETF core — broad market, small-cap value, quality, and growth — anchors the long term, while tactical single-name positions are sized deliberately and entered through tiered good-till-canceled limit orders. The discipline matters more than any single call: patience over chasing, and a plan for the exit before the entry.

Personal market views, shared for discussion — not investment advice, and not a snapshot of any account. Positioning evolves.

Fordham University, Gabelli School of Business
